ground_report: findings: Prosthesis: Right Total Knee Arthroplasty components are present. Femoral component: Clear mechanical failure with displacement/malalignment relative to the distal femur. Loss of normal alignment between femoral and tibial components. Tibial component: Significant loosening with extensive peri-prosthetic lucency and bone resorption around the tibial metaphysis and plateau. Features compatible with advanced prosthetic loosening / osteolysis. Patella & Extensor Mechanism: The patella appears deformed/disconfigured , with irregular outline. Calcifications noted along the patellar tendon and quadriceps tendon , suggesting chronic degenerative or post-surgical changes. No evidence of acute patellar fracture. Periprosthetic Bone: Marked osteolysis involving both femoral and tibial sides. No definite acute periprosthetic fracture visible on X-ray. Soft Tissues: Marked soft-tissue swelling around the knee joint. No subcutaneous gas. Alignment: Grossly abnormal knee alignment due to prosthesis failure and femoral component displacement. impressions: Mechanical failure of the right total knee prosthesis , with: Displacement of the femoral component , and Significant loosening of the tibial component with advanced peri-prosthetic osteolysis. Patellar deformity with calcifications along the patellar and quadriceps tendons , likely chronic degenerative/post-operative change. Marked soft-tissue swelling.

base_qwen: Compartments: The patellofemoral compartment shows normal joint space with no evidence of osteoarthritis or subchondral sclerosis. The tibiofemoral compartment demonstrates preserved joint space bilaterally with congruent articulation between the femoral condyles and tibial plateau. No signs of chondromalacia or cartilage loss are observed. Bone density is within normal limits without focal sclerotic or lytic lesions. No acute fractures are identified in the visualized bones including the distal femur, proximal tibia, and fibula. Loose bodies are not visualized within the joint space. Soft tissues appear unremarkable with no abnormal soft tissue masses or significant effusion noted on this projection. Impressions: Post-operative status consistent with total knee arthroplasty (TKA) with well-aligned components and no acute osseous abnormalities. Joint space preservation suggests stable implant function. No signs of loosening, periprosthetic fracture, or infection.

Compartments: The patellofemoral compartment appears clear with no evidence of osteophytes, subchondral sclerosis, or joint space narrowing suggestive of early osteoarthritis. The tibiofemoral compartment shows mild joint space narrowing consistent with moderate degenerative changes. No significant effusion is visible in either view.
Bone density: Overall bone mineral density appears preserved without obvious osteopenia or osteoporosis. No lytic or sclerotic lesions are identified in the visualized bones including distal femur, proximal tibia, fibula, and patella.
Fractures: No acute fractures are identified in the visualized long bones or joints.
Loose bodies: No intra-articular loose bodies or foreign bodies are seen within the joint spaces.
Soft tissues: Soft tissue shadows appear unremarkable with no signs of significant swelling, hematoma, or abnormal masses. No periarticular calcifications are noted.

Impressions: Degenerative joint disease of the knee with mild osteoarthritis. No acute trauma or fracture. Clear articular surfaces.
